summary: The City of Banning Police Department is seeking a qualified vendor to provide an electronic citation (eCitation) system software solution to replace their current paper citation process, enabling mobile e-ticketing, ticket management, collision reporting, and data analytics for approximately 40 users on department-issued iPhones. The technical scope encompasses eCitation mobile and desktop applications with sync services, a court export module, interfaces to the Sun Ridge Systems RIMS CAD system, integration with CHP SWITRS for collision report data uploads, a CHP-180 Tow Form mobile application, compatibility with Crossroads Software Report Writer, iOS device compatibility, Windows 10 Pro desktop client support, six mobile printers, and comprehensive project management and training. The contract structure consists of an initial one-year term with options to extend for four additional one-year terms not to exceed five years total, with pricing to remain firm during the initial term and subject to negotiation upon renewal. This procurement is funded through a Highway Safety Grant (TR26018) and requires vendors to maintain an active SAM.gov registration, comply with Title VI Civil Rights Act provisions, meet cyber insurance requirements, and support City staff with grant management obligations. The evaluation methodology weights Approach and Methodology at 30%, Qualifications at 25%, Experience at 20%, Pricing at 10%, Professional References at 10%, and Value Added Services at 5%, with top-scoring proposers potentially selected for demonstrations. Proposals must be submitted electronically through the OpenGov procurement portal by February 18, 2026, with anticipated vendor selection by March 31, 2026.
periodOfPerformance: Base year + 4 option years (not to exceed 5 years)
title: RFP-25-124 eCitation System Software
